ait: noun. An island in a river, especially the River Thames in England.
ash: noun. Solid remains of a fire.
atheist: noun. A person who does not believe in deities or gods.
att: noun. A subdivision of currency, equal to one hundredth of a Lao kip.
ease: noun. Lack of difficulty; the ability to do something easily.
east: noun. The direction of the earth's rotation, specifically 90°.
eat: verb. To ingest; to be ingested.
eath: adjective. Easy; not hard or difficult.
eats: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of eat
estate: noun. The collective property and liabilities of someone, especially a deceased person.
eta: noun. The seventh letter of the Modern Greek alphabet, the eighth in Old Greek.
eth: noun. A letter (capital Ð, small ð) introduced into Old English to represent its dental fricative, then not distinguished from the letter
haets: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of haet
has: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of have
hast: verb. second-person singular simple present indicative of have
hat: noun. A covering for the head, often in the approximate form of a cone, dome or cylinder closed at its top end, and sometimes having a brim
heats: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of heat
heist: noun. A robbery or burglary, especially from an institution such as a bank or museum.
hesitate: verb. To stop or pause respecting decision or action; to be in suspense or uncertainty as to a determination.
hie: verb. To hasten; to go quickly, to hurry.
hies: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of hie
hist: interjection. An utterance used to discreetly attract someone's attention.
sae: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Sabanê.
saith: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of say
saithe: noun. The pollock or coalfish or coley (Pollachius virens).
sat: verb. simple past and past participle of sit
sate: verb. To satisfy the appetite or desire of; to fill up.
sea: noun. A large body of salt water.
seat: noun. Something to be sat upon.
see: verb. To perceive or detect someone or something with the eyes, or as if by sight.
set: verb. To put (something) down, to rest.
sett: noun. The system of tunnels that is the home of a badger.
shat: verb. simple past and past participle of shit
she: pronoun. The female (typically) person or animal previously mentioned or implied.
shea: noun. A tree (Vitellaria paradoxa) indigenous to Africa, occurring in Mali, Cameroon, Congo, Côte d'Ivoire, Ghana, Guinea, Togo, Nigeria,
sheet: noun. A thin bed cloth used as a covering for a mattress or as a layer over the sleeper.
shit: noun. Usually solid excretory product evacuated from the bowels; feces.
sit: verb. To be in a position in which the upper body is upright and supported by the buttocks.
site: noun. The place where anything is fixed; situation; local position
state: noun. A condition; a set of circumstances applying at any given time.
stet: noun. A symbol used by proofreaders and typesetters to indicate that a word or phrase that was crossed out or changed should remain as it
tae: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tariana.
taste: noun. One of the sensations produced by the tongue in response to certain chemicals; the quality of giving this sensation.
tat: noun. Cheap and vulgar tastelessness; sleaze.
tate: noun. Of video games, a vertical mode.
tea: noun. The tea plant (Camellia sinensis); a variety of this plant.
tease: verb. To separate the fibres of (a fibrous material).
teat: noun. The projection of a mammary gland from which, on female therian mammals, milk is secreted.
tee: noun. The name of the Latin script letter T / t.
tet: noun. Alternative form of teth (“Semitic letter”).
teth: noun. The ninth letter of many Semitic alphabets/abjads (Phoenician, Aramaic, Hebrew, Syriac, Arabic and others).
that: conjunction. Introducing a clause that is the object of a verb, especially a reporting verb or verb expressing belief, knowledge,
the: article. Used before a noun phrase, including a simple noun The definite grammatical article that shows that the noun phrase that
thee: pronoun. Objective and reflexive case of thou.
theist: noun. One who believes in the existence of a god or gods.
theta: noun. The eighth letter of the Modern Greek alphabet, ninth in Old Greek: Θ, θ.
tit: noun. A person's breast or nipple.
aes: noun. plural of a, the name of the letter A.
ase: noun. An essential energy in West African religions.
ashet: noun. A large, shallow, oval dish used for serving food.
hiate: verb. To gape; to cause a hiatus.
ist: noun. A practitioner or supporter of an ism.
sai: noun. A handheld weapon with three prongs, used in some Oriental martial arts.
seah: noun. A former Hebrew unit of dry volume, about 7.7 L or 7 quarts.
staith: noun. A shore or a riverbank.
stie: noun. Obsolete form of stye (“eye infection”).
tai: noun. A fish, the Japanese bream.
tais: noun. A traditional woven cloth produced by women in East Timor.
tath: noun. The dung of livestock left on a field to serve as manure or fertiliser.
taths: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of tath
teaish: adjective. Resembling or characteristic of tea.
thees: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of thee
thetes: noun. The lowest social class of citizens in ancient Athens.
tsh: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tsuvan.
